---
created_at: 2026-04-21T10:30:00+08:00
title: Project Orion New Team Member Onboarding Guide
author: Orion Tech Backend Team
guide_effective_from: 2026-04-21
---

# Project Orion New Team Member Onboarding Guide

Welcome to Project Orion! This guide will help you get up to speed quickly with our development environment, tools, and workflows.

## Environment Setup

### Prerequisites

Before you begin, ensure you have the following installed:

- **Node.js** (v18.x or higher)
- **Python** (v3.10 or higher)
- **Docker** and **Docker Compose**
- **Git** (v2.35 or higher)
- **PostgreSQL** (v14 or higher)
- **Redis** (v7 or higher)

### Repository Setup

1. Clone the main repository:
   ```bash
   git clone git@github.com:oriontech/project-orion.git
   cd project-orion
   ```

2. Install dependencies:
   ```bash
   npm install
   pip install -r requirements.txt
   ```

3. Copy environment configuration:
   ```bash
   cp .env.example .env
   # Edit .env with your local settings
   ```

4. Start development services:
   ```bash
   docker-compose up -d postgres redis
   npm run dev
   ```

## Key Tools

### Development Tools

| Tool | Purpose | Documentation |
|------|---------|---------------|
| VS Code | Primary IDE | [Setup Guide](https://wiki.oriontech.dev/vscode) |
| Postman | API Testing | [Collection](https://postman.oriontech.dev) |
| pgAdmin | Database Management | Local instance at localhost:5050 |
| Swagger UI | API Documentation | Available at /api/docs when running locally |

### Collaboration Tools

- **Slack**: Daily communication (#backend-team, #project-orion)
- **Jira**: Sprint planning and task tracking
- **Confluence**: Technical documentation and ADRs
- **GitHub**: Code repository and pull requests
- **Figma**: UI/UX designs and mockups

### Internal Tools

- **MetaClaw Agent**: AI assistant for documentation and task management
- **Orion CLI**: Internal CLI for common development tasks
- **Log Viewer**: Centralized logging at logs.oriontech.dev

## Workflow Overview

### Sprint Cycle

Our sprints run for **2 weeks**, following this structure:

1. **Sprint Planning** (Monday, Week 1): Select stories from backlog
2. **Daily Standups** (9:30 AM CST): Quick sync on blockers and progress
3. **Mid-Sprint Review** (Friday, Week 1): Check sprint health
4. **Sprint Demo** (Thursday, Week 2): Show completed work
5. **Retrospective** (Friday, Week 2): Process improvements

### Development Workflow

1. **Pick a task** from the current sprint board
2. **Create a feature branch** from `main`:
   ```bash
   git checkout -b feature/PROJ-123-short-description
   ```
3. **Develop and test** your changes locally
4. **Submit a Pull Request** with:
   - Clear description of changes
   - Link to Jira ticket
   - Screenshots (for UI changes)
   - Test results
5. **Code Review**: At least 1 approval required from backend team
6. **Merge** after CI passes and approvals received

### Code Standards

- Follow the [Orion Style Guide](https://wiki.oriontech.dev/style-guide)
- Write unit tests for all new business logic
- Maintain >80% code coverage
- Run linting before committing: `npm run lint`
- Use conventional commit messages

## First-Week Checklist

### Day 1: Setup & Orientation

- [ ] Complete environment setup (all services running locally)
- [ ] Join Slack channels: #backend-team, #project-orion, #general
- [ ] Get access to Jira, Confluence, and GitHub
- [ ] Schedule 1:1 with Alex Zhang (Tech Lead)
- [ ] Read through Project Overview documentation
- [ ] Attend daily standup (observe)

### Day 2: Codebase Exploration

- [ ] Review the API documentation at `/api/docs`
- [ ] Explore the database schema using pgAdmin
- [ ] Run the test suite: `npm test`
- [ ] Pick up a "good first issue" from the backlog
- [ ] Complete your first code review (shadow a teammate)

### Day 3: First Contribution

- [ ] Make your first commit (documentation fix or small bug)
- [ ] Submit your first pull request
- [ ] Attend team lunch or coffee chat
- [ ] Review recent ADRs in Confluence

### Day 4: Deep Dive

- [ ] Complete technical deep-dive with assigned buddy
- [ ] Understand the data export service architecture
- [ ] Review recent sprint retrospective notes
- [ ] Set up local monitoring tools

### Day 5: Week Review

- [ ] First pull request merged!
- [ ] Complete first-week feedback survey
- [ ] Schedule check-in with Alex for next week
- [ ] Update your skills matrix in Confluence

## Getting Help

- **Technical Questions**: Ask in #backend-team Slack or tag @alex.zhang
- **Access Issues**: Contact IT at it@oriontech.dev
- **Process Questions**: Check Confluence or ask your onboarding buddy
- **Urgent Blockers**: Message Alex directly on Slack

## Resources

- [Project Wiki](https://wiki.oriontech.dev)
- [API Documentation](https://api-docs.oriontech.dev)
- [Architecture Decision Records](https://confluence.oriontech.dev/adr)
- [Team Calendar](https://calendar.oriontech.dev)

---

*Welcome to the team! We're excited to have you on board. If you have suggestions for improving this guide, please submit a PR.*
